mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.5 percent, consumer confidence stabilizes and corporate revenue growth accelerates, generating positive equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5 percent over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer spending expansion, which drives earnings revisions upward and lifts the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with 20-day forward returns in more than 40 percent of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 115 signals capacity constraints that trigger input cost inflation and margin compression, causing equity valuations to contract over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of observations where INDPRO stays above 115 for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises above 2.5 percent while FEDFUNDS remains below 3.0 percent, real interest rates turn negative, forcing capital into equities and pushing prices higher over the next month.
failure_condition: The spread signal yields a negative Spearman rank correlation with forward returns in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annually confirms robust aggregate demand, which sustains corporate profit margins and drives positive equity returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no statistically significant difference between high-GDP and low-GDP subsamples (p > 0.10) across a 15-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate validate a strengthening labor market, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, lifting equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: A two-month consecutive decline in industrial production triggers recession fears and forces analysts to downgrade earnings estimates, resulting in negative equity returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances following two consecutive months of INDPRO contraction.
